text: Alright chat, gather round, because I need to tell you about something I found. There is a cartoon from 1935 called The Sunshine Makers, sponsored by Borden Milk of all companies, and it might be the most unhinged advertisement ever created. Picture this: there are these cheerful little orange gnomes who live in a bright sunny village, and their entire job, their entire purpose in life, is bottling sunshine into little glass milk bottles. They wake up, put on their little gnome hats, and go to work bottling literal rays of sun.
label: intro

text: Meanwhile, across the way in a dark damp cave, there is a colony of sad blue goblins who are just existentially miserable. They sit around moping, frowning, generally having a terrible time. And the orange gnomes look over at them and think, you know what would fix this? Milk. Sunshine milk specifically. So they load up catapults, actual medieval siege weapons, and start launching glass bottles of liquid sunshine at the depressed goblins until they are literally forced to be happy.
label: conflict

text: The goblins drink the sunshine milk and they immediately turn orange and start dancing. Like the sadness just evaporates from their bodies and they cannot stop smiling. I am not making any of this up. Someone at Borden Milk in 1935 sat in a boardroom and said, what if we made a cartoon where our product defeats clinical depression through projectile dairy warfare? And everyone nodded and said yes, green light, here is the budget.
label: climax

text: The animation is honestly gorgeous though. The color design is brilliant, everything happy is orange and golden and warm, everything sad is blue and cold and shadowy. It is like a screensaver from 1935 that someone accidentally put a story into. The gnomes have these wonderful rubber hose limbs and the goblins are all hunched over like they have been carrying the weight of existence for centuries.
label: analysis

text: So the moral of The Sunshine Makers is apparently that the solution to all sadness is forcing happiness on people through weaponized dairy products. And honestly? I kind of respect the audacity. Seven minutes of your life, find it online, you will never look at a glass of milk the same way again. Stay sunshine out there, chat.
label: conclusion
